meta.title: Remote Control Automobiles: Hacking The Snapshot Dongle
meta.description:
Corey Thuen of Digital Bond Labs takes a look at the security and security ramifications of the Progressive Snapshot dongle that connects to a car's OBD-II port. He quickly shows the device is insecure by design like the PLC's investigated in Project Basecamp. By this we mean the Snapshot dongle is missing even the most basic security controls. An attacker does not need to defeat security to attack the dongle and then the automobile.

Corey also found that the dongle was written without even the most basic secure coding practices. So if an attacker wanted to find and exploit vulnerabilities, which is not necessary today, it would not be difficult.

The second half of the talk looks at some basic and advanced things an attacker might do with access to a car's CANBus.
